claim_status: Benchmark statistics-analysis contract only; no browser benchmark run, no runner-generated raw sample, no statistical summary from real samples, no confidence interval from measured browser data, no benchmark result, no competitor result, no memory result, no energy result, no Chrome-class claim, no faster claim, no lower-memory claim, no lower-energy claim, no public performance claim, no production claim, and no daily-driver claim.

required_inputs.missing_proof: No browser benchmark runner has produced raw samples, sample order, warmup samples, or failure records.
The checked trace package contract exists, but no runner-generated ETW, Perfetto-compatible trace, memory, power, log, or artifact-index package exists.
No fixed-hardware noise study exists to set repetitions, thresholds, confidence intervals, or practical significance.
No browser-run denominator records exist for unsupported, failed, timed-out, cancelled, crashed, discarded, revived, wrong-target, cleanup, or trace-finalization cases.
No owner, performance, benchmark-operations, quality, security, accessibility, or release-operations review has accepted statistical methods or claim scope.

sample_design_controls: declare measured sample count per browser, scenario, hardware tier, cache state, and tab-lifecycle condition before a run is interpreted
retain warmup policy, warmup sample count, measured sample order, and whether warmup samples are excluded from summaries
record randomization or paired-run order so Turing and competitor runs do not receive systematic thermal, cache, update, or clock-order advantages
retain cold, warm, repeat-view, cache, profile, network, display, power, thermal, and background-service state beside every sample
retain failed, unsupported, timed-out, cancelled, crashed, discarded, revived, state-loss, wrong-target, trace-finalization, cleanup, and profile-isolation cases in the denominator
separate mixed-state 30-tab and all-live isolation-stress samples so hidden discarding cannot masquerade as equivalent active-page performance

statistical_methods.method: measure baseline variance on the selected hardware, OS image, browser pins, corpus, and runner before accepting thresholds
derive repetitions from noise, metric family, failure rate, and practical impact instead of using a single arbitrary run
statistical_methods.missing_proof: No sample-size rationale exists for browser-run metrics.

statistical_methods.method: report uncertainty statements or confidence intervals for medians, percentiles, means where used, ratios, and differences
statistical_methods.missing_proof: No measured browser-run dataset exists for interval estimation.

statistical_methods.method: report effect size and practical significance alongside statistical significance
declare outlier, retry, cancellation, crash, and invalid-run policy before running, then retain rejected samples in the artifact bundle
statistical_methods.missing_proof: No browser-run outlier or retry policy has been executed.

statistical_methods.method: account for multiple metrics, browsers, scenarios, and hardware tiers when interpreting public comparisons
statistical_methods.missing_proof: No comparison analysis plan has been owner-reviewed.

metric_families.failure_policy: Timeout, cancellation, crash, unsupported, wrong-target, and cleanup failures stay in the denominator.
Discarded, frozen, shared, compressed, swapped, GPU, unknown memory states, and memory-accounting failure cases remain visible in the denominator instead of collapsed into one favorable number.
Power-source, thermal, display, background-service, GPU, local-model, missing-counter, and energy-counter failure cases stay visible in the denominator.
Mixed-state and all-live runs are reported separately; hidden discard, state loss, protected-tab override, crash, and revival failure are denominator events.
Unsupported pages, failed pages, suite subtests, and compatibility failure cases cannot be dropped from claim denominators.

denominator_controls: publish denominator size for every browser, corpus, suite, scenario, hardware tier, and cache state
publish unsupported behavior, failed cases, omitted cases, invalidated samples, and residual risk

block comparisons when unsupported cases, security settings, lifecycle settings, profile state, cache state, extension state, account state, process topology, or benchmark suite versions differ outside the reviewed claim scope
retain raw artifacts and hashes for samples that are excluded from a summary
treat validation, no-claim templates, no-browser self-tests, smoke output, and diagnostic browser-pin captures as planning evidence only

rejection_rules.condition: Runner-generated raw samples, sample order, warmup records, or raw-artifact hashes are missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject benchmark result, public performance, Chrome-class, faster, lower-memory, lower-energy, competitor-result, production, and daily-driver claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Noise study, sample-size rationale, confidence interval, uncertainty statement, effect size, or practical-significance threshold is missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject public comparison and performance-claim language.

rejection_rules.condition: Unsupported, failed, timed-out, cancelled, crashed, discarded, revived, state-loss, wrong-target, trace-finalization, cleanup, or profile-isolation cases are hidden or dropped.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject benchmark-ready status and public claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Hardware, OS image, driver, firmware, display, power, thermal, browser version, security setting, sandbox, site isolation, cache, profile, account, extension, lifecycle, route, viewport, or suite version differs outside reviewed scope.
This contract, schema validation, no-browser self-tests, no-claim templates, smoke runner output, or diagnostic browser-pin captures are cited as benchmark results.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject all benchmark, product, production, and implementation claims.

unsupported_behavior: no confidence interval from measured browser data exists
no public performance, production, release, stable, beta, daily-driver, or implementation claim is supported
